---
title: "Marginalia"
subtitle: "Grounded QA for Technical Documents"
author: "Zeno F."
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, marginalia-theme.scss]
    transition: fade
    transition-speed: slow
    slide-number: c/t
    progress: true
    logo: ""
    footer: "Marginalia · Interview Project"
    highlight-style: github
    code-block-bg: true
    code-block-border-left: "#3E7F6A"
    incremental: false
    width: 1200
    height: 700
    margin: 0.08
    auto-animate: true
    auto-animate-easing: ease-in-out
    auto-animate-duration: 0.6
engine: markdown
---

## {background-color="#F6F2E9" .title-custom}

::: {.title-block}
::: {.title-eyebrow}
Interview Project
:::

# Marginalia

::: {.title-subtitle}
Grounded question-answering for technical and scientific PDFs.
:::

::: {.title-meta}
Zeno F. · Shortlisted Interview · 2026
:::
:::

::: {.title-deco}
:::

---

## The Problem {auto-animate=true}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}

::: {.problem-block}
**Technical PDFs contain useful knowledge.**  
Querying them reliably is still difficult.
:::

::: {.fragment}
::: {.pain-points}
- documents have structure, not just plain text
- keyword search misses semantic meaning
- LLMs hallucinate without retrieved evidence
- users need citations and page-level provenance
:::
:::

:::
::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.side-quote}
**Design goal**

Build a retrieval-augmented QA system that answers from document evidence and shows exactly where the answer came from.
:::
:::
:::

---

## What is Marginalia? {auto-animate=true}

::: {.definition-card}
Marginalia is a **grounded RAG system** for technical and scientific documents, with structure-aware ingestion, retrieval quality controls, and citation-backed answers.
:::

::: {.columns .feature-row}
::: {.column width="33%"}
::: {.feature-pill}
📄 **Ingest**  
Docling parsing with metadata
:::
:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
::: {.feature-pill}
🧠 **Retrieve**  
Embeddings, Qdrant, reranking
:::
:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
::: {.feature-pill}
💬 **Answer**  
Evidence, citations, API
:::
:::
:::

---

## Architecture {auto-animate=true}

```text
PDF
-> Docling parsing
-> structured chunks + metadata
-> sentence-transformers embeddings
-> Qdrant retrieval
-> cross-encoder reranking
-> evidence blocks
-> OpenAI answer generation
-> cited sources + API response
```

::: {.arch-note}
FastAPI exposes `/index` and `/ask`. LangChain structures prompts and chat messages. LangGraph models the answer workflow as explicit nodes.
:::

---

## Results & Benchmarks {auto-animate=true}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.metric-card}
::: {.metric-number}
1.00
:::
::: {.metric-label}
Recall@5 on the current retrieval benchmark
:::
:::

::: {.metric-card}
::: {.metric-number}
139
:::
::: {.metric-label}
Chunks indexed from the sample paper
:::
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.metric-card}
::: {.metric-number}
2
:::
::: {.metric-label}
API endpoints: `/index` and `/ask`
:::
:::

::: {.metric-card}
::: {.metric-number}
4
:::
::: {.metric-label}
LangGraph nodes in the QA workflow
:::
:::
:::
:::

---

## What I Learned {auto-animate=true}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.learned-block .fragment}
**Document structure matters**  
Technical PDFs are not just text blobs. Preserving section, page, and chunk metadata is what makes grounded answers possible.
:::

::: {.learned-block .fragment}
**Retrieval quality matters as much as the LLM**  
Vector retrieval gets candidates quickly, but reranking and query rewriting noticeably improve the evidence quality passed to generation.
:::
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.learned-block .fragment}
**Framework boundaries matter**  
I used LangChain for prompt orchestration and LangGraph for workflow structure, but kept retrieval and evaluation explicit in application code.
:::

::: {.learned-block .fragment}
**Evaluation keeps the system honest**  
I added retrieval evaluation with `recall@5` because fluent answers alone are not enough to judge a RAG pipeline.
:::
:::
:::

---

## What's Next {auto-animate=true}

::: {.roadmap}
::: {.roadmap-item .done}
✅ Ingestion, indexing, retrieval, grounded QA API
:::
::: {.roadmap-item .done}
✅ Reranking, request-level memory, query rewriting
:::
::: {.roadmap-item .active}
🔄 Broader evaluation beyond the current benchmark
:::
::: {.roadmap-item}
⬜ Multi-document benchmarking and comparison
:::
::: {.roadmap-item}
⬜ Richer LangGraph branching and fallback logic
:::
:::

::: {.cta-block}
Reliable LLM systems depend as much on retrieval, metadata, and evaluation as on the model itself.
:::
